behaviors.behavior: is_cross_component_change True on merge/churn/gitattributes/resolver + hooks.json + (nested) hook scripts + verify_phase + campaign_*
verifier FAILs: medium+ cross-component diff with NO category:integration behavior
verifier OK when a category:integration behavior is present
verifier OK when the diff is not cross-component
verifier RECOMPUTES from the diff, not an agent-reported flag (anti-dodge)
verifier returns a DISTINCT failure on a corrupt results.json (not 'missing coverage')
SKILL risk-taxonomy + phase-matrix + Step-7.5 + F5 + confidence-anti-patterns advertise the flag
Kern SKILL.md stays <=300 LOC after the additions

surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal risk-gate meta-tooling: a new diff-driven risk flag (classify_complexity) + a non-dodgeable F11 verifier check (iterate_checks) + runtime-prompt prose. No application surface. The detector is unit-tested; the verifier gate is driven through REAL git diffs (merge-base..HEAD) against bare-origin worktrees incl. the anti-dodge recompute path; the SSoT drift + prose contracts are pinned. The gate's own meta-tooling is deliberately excluded from the patterns (no circular self-gating).
